message:
test(ft-kernel-cpu): lock log_softmax precision regression for [bd-ebrb]

Adds log_softmax_preserves_precision_at_large_magnitudes regression
test directly at the kernel level. Asserts <= 16 ULP absolute
agreement against the analytical scipy reference values for
x = [1000, 1001, 1002]:
  expected = [-2.4076059644443804, -1.4076059644443804, -0.4076059644443804]

The pre-fix kernel returned -2.4076059644444285 etc. (~1000 ULPs
off) due to the (max + log(sum_exp)) intermediate collapsing two
~1000-magnitude values into a ~-O(1) result via subtraction. The
post-fix kernel computes (x[i] - max) - log(sum_exp) and returns
the analytical values bit-exactly.

This complements the higher-level
torch_softmax_scipy_subprocess_conformance harness (which catches
the same bug via scipy oracle) by locking the kernel directly so
a kernel-level rewrite that breaks precision can't slip through
even if the conformance harness is skipped (e.g. python3 / scipy
unavailable).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
